title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
statement_text: Every graph of chromatic number at least $r$ has $K_r$ as a minor.
context_text: Proposed by Hadwiger in 1943, this conjecture is known for $r \leq 5$ but remains widely open for $r \geq 6$. The paper's entire research program — bounding the size of a clique minor under structural restrictions — is motivated by Hadwiger's conjecture and the difficulty of proving it in full generality.
paper_contribution: Proves that any $K_s$-free $n$-vertex graph with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ has a clique minor of order at least $(n/r)^{1+\frac{1}{10(s-2)}}$ (Theorem 1), which substantially exceeds the bound $n/\alpha(G)$ that Hadwiger's conjecture would imply for those graphs.
paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph

title: Clique minor conjecture for bounded independence number
statement_text: If $\alpha(G) \leq r$ then $G$ has a clique minor of order $n/r$.
context_text: Stated as 'an old conjecture' implied by Hadwiger's conjecture (see reference [28] of the paper). Duchet and Meyniel proved it holds within a factor of 2 in 1982, with subsequent improvements by Fox, Balogh–Kostochka, and others. The paper's main result gives a polynomially stronger bound for $K_s$-free graphs.
paper_contribution: For $K_s$-free graphs proves the stronger bound $(n/r)^{1+\frac{1}{10(s-2)}}$ on the clique minor order (Theorem 1), going well beyond the $n/r$ bound this conjecture asserts.
notes: Original authors not named in the extracted text; attributed only to reference [28].
paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph

title: Question of Dvořák and Yepremyan on $K_s$-free graphs
statement_text: Does every $K_s$-free $n$-vertex graph $G$ with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ (with $n/r$ large enough) have a clique minor of order polynomially larger than $n/r$?
context_text: Dvořák and Yepremyan proved that triangle-free graphs with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ have a clique minor of order $(n/r)^{1+\frac{1}{26}}$, answering a question of Norin. They then asked whether the same phenomenon holds for $K_s$-free graphs for arbitrary fixed $s \geq 3$.
paper_contribution: Answers the question affirmatively by proving (Theorem 1) that any $K_s$-free $n$-vertex graph with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ has a clique minor of order at least $(n/r)^{1+\frac{1}{10(s-2)}}$, provided $n/r$ is large enough; the exponent is tight up to a constant factor.
paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph

title: Informal conjecture on the generality of the probabilistic minor strategy
statement_text: The strategy introduced in this paper, or minor modifications of it, could provide a useful tool for finding clique minors in graphs under other structural restrictions as well.
context_text: After showing that the same randomised star-contraction strategy yields both Theorem 1 (for $K_s$-free graphs with bounded independence number) and a short proof of the Kühn–Osthus result for $K_{s,t}$-free graphs, the authors note that the approach performs well in two quite different structural settings.
paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph
